name: Self-State Attacks on Self-Hosted AI Agents: How Far Can OS Defenses Go?
summary: arXiv:2607.17986v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Self-hosted AI agents read and write their own memory and configuration files to function. An agent may get compromised via corruption of its own s...
description: arXiv:2607.17986v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Self-hosted AI agents read and write their own memory and configuration files to function. An agent may get compromised via corruption of its own state -- a compromise realized via legitimate OS system call invocation. We refer to this class of threats as self-state attacks. In this paper, we investigate the OS resilience to this class of attack...
author: Yimeng Chen, Nathana\"el Denis, Roberto Di Pietro, J\"urgen Schmidhuber
